title: Where is the justice? Nothing is missed #shorts
description:
How does the Qur'an frame tragedy of the highest order? Why does triumph not always manifest within the lifetimes of those who walk the path of truth? Since Allah is capable of all things, why does oppression persist? Surah Al-Buruj, chapter 85 of the Qur'an, provides balance, context and relief to anxious hearts.

title: Commitment to the phone charger #shorts
description:
Episode 1: https://youtu.be/vLb4YF-0F5M

Why do some hearts crumble under minor inconveniences, while others endure immense loss and even grow in strength? This video investigates another action of heart - al-Inābah: the soul’s constant return to its Lord, and why it is the key to many of life's endless challenges.

title: Episode 20: Repentance (Tawbah) | Change of Heart Series Final Episode
description:
Shayṭān is persistent, desires are powerful, the soul is weak, and one is always in need of a door back to Allah. Tawbah (repentance) is that door that isn't only for those who've “lost their way”. It is the daily work of every true believer. It is the proof that a person still wants Allah, as well as the proof that Allah still wants His servant.

title: Episode 19: Fear (Al-Khawf) | Change of Heart Series
description:
Fear is one of the greatest engines of human movement. A man may run fast when he is chasing a prize. Put a medal before him, a trophy, a promotion, a dream and he will discover speed, as hope pulls one forward. But place a lion behind him, and suddenly another speed appears, a speed he did not know lived in his legs. Sharpness enters his senses and all distractions fall away. That is the driving power of fear.

title: Episode 18: Hope (Al-Rajā’) | Change of Heart Series
description:
Episode 19: https://youtu.be/Gpx0umchMmY

If human life could be summed up in one phrase, it would be this: unfinished business. Every one of us is carrying something unresolved: a sin we want Allah to erase, a habit we are trying to break, a wound we are waiting for Him to heal, a deed we are begging Him to accept, or a better version of ourselves we hope He allows us to become. What keeps us moving towards all of it is al-rajāʾ: hope.

title: Relief is perfectly timed #shorts
description:
The atrocities that people inflict on one another is tough to comprehend and, at times, is overwhelming, particularly when children are involved. Such behaviour can easily shake the faith of many, leading them to question the very basics of belief. Here’s a practical four-part approach to coping with tragedy we witness on others or experience ourselves.

description:

title: Episode 15: Contentment (Al-Riḍā, Part 2) | Change of Heart Series
description:
Episode 16: https://youtu.be/UTkh9vU5LAs

Why do some people stay at peace while their life falls apart, whilst others with everything still feel empty? In this episode of the Change of Heart series, we Ridā, contentment with whatever Allah decrees.
Through the stories of Hajar in the desert, the Prophet's own losses, and Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas choosing blindness over complaint, he shows what real contentment looks like under pressure, not passive resignation, but a settled heart.
We outline what it brings: forgiveness, the sweetness of faith, dignity, the good life and ultimately Allah's pleasure, then closes with practical steps, saying alhamdulillah through hardship, dropping comparison, remembering death, dua, and truly knowing Allah.

A grounding watch for anyone exhausted by chasing more.

Episode 1 of the series: https://youtu.be/vLb4YF-0F5M

title: Episode 14: Contentment (Al-Riḍā, Part 1) | Change of Heart Series
description:
The modern machine feeds on discontent, as it is precisely what keeps people consuming, scrolling, chasing, and never arriving. Here, Islam calls the believer to something far higher: al-riḍā, serene contentment with Allah.

We explore: How does riḍā differ from ṣabr (patience), tawakkul (reliance), and shukr (gratitude)? And what are the signs that a heart is truly content with Allah?

Episode 15: https://youtu.be/MtdfwgVXeP0

title: The Worship of Letting Go
description:
The believer’s greatest profits, particularly when placed at the crossroad of life, are not always in what he manages to keep or multiply, but in what he's able to release for Allah.

title: The Overwhelming Sway of the Qur'an
description:
What is it about the Qur’an that seizes a room the moment it’s recited? There is a strange, overwhelming sway it holds over the soul, a dominion that minds struggle to explain but hearts cannot deny. What you’re feeling in those moments is the Qur’an laying its hand on your soul - a real, palpable serenity that countless people have tried to describe, and always end up doing so with the same mix of wonder and bewilderment.

title: The Ache of Too Late | Episode 16 | Tomorrow As though You can See it FINAL Episode
description:
One man would offer the entire earth to escape Hell, and it would not be accepted. Another is saved by a single card bearing lā ilāha illa Allāh. Tawḥīd makes nothing too heavy, and shirk makes nothing heavy enough.
